title: Combination Sum
description: Given an array of candidates and a target target, return all unique combinations of candidates where the chosen numbers sum to target. You may use the same number from candidates an unlimited number of times. The solution set must not contain duplicate combinations.

examples.explanation: The combinations that sum to 7 are [2, 2, 3] and [7]. Other combinations such as [3, 4] do not exist since 4 is not in the candidates.
The combinations that sum to 8 include [2, 2, 2, 2], [2, 3, 3], and [3, 5]. Each combination is unique and sums up to the target.
The only combination that sums to 1 is [1] itself. There are no other candidates to consider.

hints: Consider using backtracking to explore all possible combinations.
You can start from each candidate and recursively try to reach the target.
Ensure that you handle duplicates by managing the starting index for candidates.
Think about how to efficiently manage the recursion and backtrack when necessary.
